name: First American Bank
description_short: Full-service bank offering checking, savings, mortgages, business banking, and wealth management across Illinois, Florida, and Wisconsin locations.
description_long:
First American Bank is a regional bank with a multi-state presence serving personal, business, and wealth clients. The bank operates branches in Illinois, Florida, and Wisconsin, positioning itself as a community-focused financial institution. Founded with a commitment to personalized banking, First American Bank has expanded its service offerings significantly over time to compete with larger national banks while maintaining regional presence and expertise.

The bank provides comprehensive personal banking services including checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit. On the lending side, First American offers mortgages, home equity loans, construction loans, bridge loans, and foreign national lending. For business clients, the bank provides commercial checking, business loans, SBA loans, commercial real estate financing, and merchant services. Their wealth management division includes investment management, private banking, trust and estate services, and retirement plan administration.

First American Bank distinguishes itself through specialized services not commonly offered by smaller regional banks, including foreign national lending, U.S. banking for foreign corporations, ESOP financing, and treasury management solutions. The bank actively markets limited-time promotional offers such as CD specials and home equity line of credit introductory rates. They also operate a rewards program tied to debit card usage and account referrals, and offer unique features like Health Savings Accounts and Zelle money transfer integration.

As a regional bank, First American operates with geographic limitations (three-state footprint) compared to national banks, which may limit convenience for customers outside their service areas. The website shows comprehensive service offerings, but specific rate competitiveness, fee structures, and customer service quality cannot be fully assessed from website content alone. The bank appears well-positioned for customers seeking personalized service and specialized lending products, though those prioritizing maximum branch convenience or the lowest advertised rates should compare options. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts with overdraft protection and debit cards
Savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit
Mortgages with $500 closing cost credit promotion
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Business checking and business money market accounts
Business loans, SBA loans, and commercial real estate financing
Merchant services and card processing for businesses
Wealth advisory, investment management, and private banking
Trust and estate services including charitable trusts and estate guardianship
Health Savings Accounts and Individual Retirement Accounts
pros: Offers specialized lending products including foreign national lending, bridge loans, and construction loans not widely available at all banks
Provides comprehensive wealth management services including trust and estate planning, charitable trust services, and retirement plan administration
Features a rewards program allowing customers to earn points on debit card purchases and account referrals with redeemable rewards
Offers $500 mortgage closing cost credit for customers who set up automatic loan payments and eStatements
Provides Health Savings Accounts with specialized guidance for healthcare-focused savers
Offers Treasury Management and Business Fraud Prevention services for commercial customers
Competitive CD rates advertised (7-Month CD at 3.40% APY, 11-Month CD at 3.65% APY at time of review)
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Illinois, Florida, and Wisconsin, making access inconvenient for customers outside these states
Website does not display specific fee schedules, minimum balance requirements, or detailed rate information for most products
No mention of mobile app capabilities beyond basic mobile wallets and Zelle integration, limiting digital-first banking experience assessment
Limited transparency on business loan terms, SBA loan specifics, or commercial real estate financing details without direct contact
No information provided about account FDIC insurance coverage limits or specific deposit protections

contact.address: 700 Busse Rd, Elk Grove Village, IL 60007

best_for: Business owners and entrepreneurs seeking specialized lending like SBA loans, ESOP financing, or commercial real estate loans
High-net-worth individuals and families wanting comprehensive wealth management, trust services, and investment advisory
Customers in Illinois, Florida, or Wisconsin seeking personalized banking relationships with local branch access
Foreign nationals or international businesses needing U.S. banking services and foreign currency exchange capabilities
diagnosis: First American Bank is best for customers in its three-state service area (IL, FL, WI) seeking comprehensive banking with specialized lending products and wealth management services. The main caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside these states cannot access branches or services, and those seeking the simplest, lowest-cost banking may find better value at online-only neobanks or larger national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 700 Busse Rd, Elk Grove Village, IL 60007
